markets.question: Will Delcy Rodríguez be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in London be 5°C on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 8°C (43%) and 9°C (39%) for London City Airport's lowest temperature on May 10, driven by the Met Office's latest hourly forecast—updated evening of May 9—projecting overnight drops to 8–10°C amid persistent cloud cover and northeasterly winds up to 15 mph that inhibit radiative cooling by trapping heat and mixing boundary-layer air. Observations already logged 10°C at 04:00 BST, aligning with early cloud-trapped warmth, while May 8's analogous 8°C minimum reinforces this range. Lower odds for 7°C (17%) hinge on potential wind-lightening or partial clearing allowing greater nocturnal cooling, though model ensembles show low confidence in such shifts; real-time EGLC station data through evening will resolve uncertainties typical of short-range minimum forecasts.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 35m and 40m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures (GC) for June 2026 contracts trade around $4,725 per ounce, capturing trader consensus on gold's safe-haven demand amid resurgent inflation and a cautious Federal Reserve. markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democrat April McClain Delaney leads trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability to win Maryland's 6th Congressional District House seat, reflecting the district's Safe Democratic rating from Sabato's Crystal Ball and Cook Political Report amid a partisan lean favoring Democrats by wide margins in recent cycles. A Public Sentiment Institute poll from April 8-11 showed Delaney at 55% against a generic Republican at 31%, with challenger David Trone polling even stronger at 59%, underscoring Democratic dominance regardless of the June 23 primary outcome between the heavily self-funded rivals. Weak Republican fundraising—top GOP candidates under $310,000 raised—and historical incumbency advantages in this suburban-rural district sustain low odds for Republicans at 9%, though national midterm dynamics could introduce volatility ahead of November 3.

markets.question: Spread: Rangers FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Scottish Premiership game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Rangers FC" if Rangers FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Hibernian FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on spfl.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Rangers FC vs. Hibernian FC - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: Rangers host Hibernian at Ibrox in the Scottish Premiership Championship Round on May 13, amid a fiercely contested title race where Hearts lead with 70 points from 33 games, Rangers sit second on 69, and Celtic trail on 67. Rangers' trader consensus reflects urgency after a shocking 2-3 home loss to Motherwell on April 26—snapping momentum from their 6-3 thrashing of Falkirk—while Hibs, fifth in the table, fell 1-2 to Hearts that weekend. Rangers boast an unbeaten run in recent head-to-heads (three wins, one draw since October), strong Ibrox form, but face injury concerns with Ryan Naderi sidelined long-term and Tuur Rommens doubtful; Hibs monitor Josh Mulligan's ankle recovery. Late-season stakes amplify pressure for the hosts.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Cleveland Fed nowcasts signal April CPI month-over-month gains around 0.4%, potentially pushing headline rates toward 3.8-4%, reflecting trader consensus on Polymarket for a 2026 peak exceeding prior Fed projections of 2.7% median PCE. Robust labor markets and persistent shelter costs underpin sticky core pressures, though base effects may ease later. Key catalysts include tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 and May PCE data on May 28, ahead of the June FOMC where rate cut odds hinge on inflation trajectory.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Ongoing appeals in his insurrection trial, with hearings as recent as May 7, show no bail grants or release signals, amid procedural hurdles like extended detention warrants that historically prolong high-profile cases. Late-breaking acquittals or health-related pardons could shift odds, though none appear imminent.

markets.question: Will Arsenal score the most goals in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/clubs/goals/).

Ensemble means from ECMWF and GFS cluster near 12°C, but significant spread—driven by variable cloud cover, boundary layer mixing, and timing of potential precipitation—differentiates outcomes like 11–13°C, all trading near 20%. Watch for 00Z model updates overnight and KNMI refinements tomorrow, as small shifts in insolation could tip the balance.

markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

events.title: What will Fed Rate hit before 2027?
events.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve has held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% since its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, driven by reaccelerating inflation—March CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year and PCE 3.5%—coupled with a resilient labor market showing April nonfarm payrolls up 115,000 and unemployment unchanged at 4.3%. The March dot plot signals trader consensus for gradual easing to a median 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027, consistent with CME FedWatch-implied paths favoring near-term holds. Polymarket reflects this skin-in-the-game sentiment, with key catalysts ahead including the June 16-17 FOMC, May CPI data, and payrolls that could shift rate cut expectations before year-end 2027.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Erling Haaland's commanding four-goal lead in the Premier League Golden Boot race, now at 26 goals to Igor Thiago's 22, drives trader consensus to 98.3% implied probability after Haaland's 75th-minute strike in Manchester City's 3-0 win over Brentford yesterday, extending his advantage from two goals in this pivotal head-to-head. With three matches remaining until the May 24 finale, City's title-contending form and favorable fixtures bolster Haaland's starts and service, while mid-table Brentford faces tougher tests for Thiago despite his hot streak of three goals in five recent outings. Realistic challenges include a late Haaland injury or suspension, or Thiago netting four-plus while Haaland blanks amid rotation risks.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Released in mid-April, the model excels in multi-file reasoning, bug fixing, and real-world software engineering, per official evaluations and third-party leaderboards, outshining OpenAI's GPT-5.4 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro. While uncertainty persists—potential GPT-5.5 launches or Google I/O surprises could narrow the gap—Anthropic's multi-model dominance (multiple Claudes in top ranks) and lack of recent challenger breakthroughs underpin the strong positioning, with resolution hinging on late-May updates.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's highly anticipated ninth studio album, Iceman, set for release on May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records, anchors trader consensus in this market betting on key lyrical mentions across its tracks. Recent teasers—including virtual icing of his Toronto mansion, cryptic ice-block stunts unveiling the date, and Adin Ross hinting at fatherhood themes and features—have fueled expectations of autobiographical nods to "Daddy," hometown pride in Toronto, pandemic callbacks via "Covid," and potential jabs at rivals like Kendrick Lamar amid their lingering beef shadow. With Episode 4 livestream tomorrow (May 14) as the final pre-drop catalyst, resolution hinges on Spotify or Apple Music transcripts post-streaming debut, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in parsing Drake's signature blend of personal lore and cultural disses before Billboard charts confirm dominance.

markets.question: Will Ben Griffin win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex Fitzpatrick holds a one-shot 54-hole lead at 14-under par entering the final round of the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, with Kristoffer Reitan one back at 13-under and Cameron Young two strokes off the pace at 12-under, fueled by their sizzling third-round scores of 64, 64, and a season-low 63, respectively. This tight leaderboard reflects trader consensus on a bunched race among players chasing their first PGA Tour victory, as Fitzpatrick surges with eight birdies in round three, Reitan dazzles in his Quail Hollow debut via hot putting, and Young leverages strong course history amid multiple near-misses. Lurking challengers like Nicolai Hojgaard and Sung-Jae Im sit five back at 10-under, while big names such as Rory McIlroy faded dramatically, amplifying the leaders' edge on the demanding par-71 layout amid mild final-round conditions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aubry Bracco commands an 86.5% implied probability as the Survivor 50 frontrunner, propelled by her breakout winner's edit and pivotal role in Episode 11's soul-crushing blindside, where Ozzy Lusth's idol fumble and self-sabotage handed her key jury management leverage amid a prize-doubling twist and split Tribal chaos. Traders, whose markets have nailed prior eliminations, see her late-game momentum—rising from under-the-radar to resume-builder—as unassailable against Cirie Fields' 7.2% bid, fueled by the legend's game management but growing target status. markets.question: Map Handicap: GL (-1.5) vs Sinners (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Sinners and GamerLegion in the IEM Atlanta Group B, initially scheduled for May 11 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion wins 2 or more maps than Sinners in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Sinners". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Sinners vs GamerLegion (BO3) - IEM Atlanta Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Sinners and GamerLegion in the IEM Atlanta Group B, initially scheduled for May 11 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Sinners" if Sinners win the match against GamerLegion.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion win the match against Sinners.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: GamerLegion enters as slight trader favorite over SINNERS in their IEM Atlanta 2026 Group B BO3 opener, buoyed by a 2-1 head-to-head edge including a 2-0 win in February's Parken Challenger Championship on Inferno and Nuke. Holding HLTV #31 ranking to SINNERS' #32, GamerLegion boasts a 70% win rate over the past three months, highlighted by a recent 2-1 upset over FURIA in BLAST Rivals last week before a 0-2 loss to top-ranked Natus Vincere on May 1. SINNERS rides a three-match win streak from LORGAR Rankings against lower-tier foes like Gentle Mates and KOLESIE in late April, with strong Nuke (80% win rate) and Mirage (67%) stats mirroring GamerLegion's strengths. Stable rosters for both—GamerLegion's REZ, Tauson, hypex, PR, Snax; SINNERS' SHOCK, MoDo, beastik, kisserek, stressarN—face a high-stakes double-elimination group alongside NAVI, Liquid, and Astralis, where map vetoes on the active pool including Dust2, Overpass, Ancient, and Anubis could prove decisive amid no major roster disruptions.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Where will Trump and Putin meet next?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30, driven by the absence of any official announcements or scheduled bilateral summits in the near term, despite a 1.5-hour phone call on April 29 discussing Ukraine truce prospects and an imminent resolution deal. The White House invitation for Putin to attend the G20 summit in Miami this December—far beyond the resolution window—comes with Trump expressing doubts on attendance, underscoring ongoing logistical barriers including Putin's ICC arrest warrant limiting travel options and preference for remote diplomacy amid the protracted Ukraine conflict. Realistic challenges to this outcome would require a sudden diplomatic breakthrough, such as accelerated peace negotiations prompting an urgent summit in a neutral venue like Turkey or a Gulf country.

markets.question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including Ideia (May 1-5) and Real Time Big Data (May 2-4), show President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading first-round voting intentions at 40% against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 34-36%, yet Flávio holds slim edges in simulated runoffs (44-45% to Lula's 43-45%), driving trader consensus to a near-tie with Flávio at 41.3% implied probability. This tightness stems from Flávio's consolidation of right-wing votes following shifts like São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' gubernatorial pivot, offsetting Lula's incumbency amid 51% disapproval ratings and mutual high rejection rates exceeding 44%. The polarized race awaits party conventions by late May, economic updates, and scandals that could sway undecided voters or force coalitions before the October 4 first round.

markets.question: Will Estonia be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the named country finishes Eurovision 2026 as one of the top 3 highest scoring candidates.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund follow closely, buoyed by strong televote potential and jury appeal from recent previews, while Italy's Sal Da Vinci climbs on vocal delivery. With solo rehearsals complete and full dress runs starting May 11 ahead of semi-finals on May 12 and 14 in Vienna, qualification results and live televote-jury splits will be pivotal swing factors before the May 16 final.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 46.5% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated "Project Apex" IPO, driven by its longstanding ties to Elon Musk—including top banker Michael Grimes' return—and recent reports of E*Trade negotiating a prominent retail investor allocation role. Goldman Sachs (17.5%) and Bank of America (14.0%) trail as fellow senior underwriters in a 21-bank syndicate confirmed in early April meetings led by Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen, targeting a $75 billion raise at $1.75 trillion valuation with 30% retail shares. Lower odds for JPMorgan and others reflect less prominent positioning amid no traditional "lead left" designation. Key catalysts include the S-1 prospectus expected late May and roadshow starting June 8, amid SpaceX's dominance in reusable rockets and Starlink expansion.

markets.question: Nvidia Data Center Revenue above 50B in Q1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Nvidia’s data center revenue for the first fiscal quarter of 2027, as reported in its official company earnings materials, is above the listed amount. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.
The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

If the specified metric is reported as a range rather than a specific number, the midpoint of the range will be used for resolution of this market. 

The resolution source for this market is Nvidia’s official company earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, and regulatory filings. If the specified metric is not reported in these materials, recordings or transcripts of the company’s earnings webcast may also be used.

Note: This market will resolve based on the most numerically precise version of the specified metric reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Only the specified metric will be considered; alternate versions that differ in definition or scope from the specified metric will not be considered.

Management's $78 billion (±2%) total revenue guidance—explicitly excluding China data center compute amid U.S. export curbs—implies sequential acceleration, with analysts projecting data center sales around $70 billion fueled by Blackwell GPU ramp-up and hyperscaler AI capex surges. Key watchpoints include gross margins (guided 74.9%) and Q2 outlook; earnings release on May 20 could trigger volatility based on guidance beats versus Street estimates.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 35°C or higher on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a highest temperature of 34°C at Kuala Lumpur International Airport Station today, aligning with official Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) forecasts capping the max at 34°C amid isolated afternoon thunderstorms that suppress further heating. Current observations confirm peaks of 34°C under partly cloudy skies, consistent with May's tropical monsoon climatology where average highs hover around 32–33°C but can nudge higher on clearer mornings. UK Met Office models echo this 34°C daytime maximum with 30% rain chances. Realistic challenges include unexpected prolonged sunshine into late afternoon, potentially driving brief spikes above 35°C, though forecast consensus and cloud cover make this improbable as the day progresses toward evening data finalization.

markets.question: Will Algeria vs. Austria end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 27, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Natus Vincere vs Bigetron by Vitality (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Natus Vincere and Bigetron by Vitality in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 6:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Bigetron by Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Bigetron by Vitality" if Bigetron by Vitality win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

eventMetadata.context_description: Swapped commands a commanding 98.4% implied probability as the top US Netflix movie this week, fueled by its rapid ascent to #1 on daily streaming charts following a blockbuster premiere on May 1. The Skydance Animation family adventure, directed by Nathan Greno and featuring voices like Michael B. Jordan and Juno Temple, racked up 15.5 million views in its first three days—Netflix's strongest animated opening this year—propelled by word-of-mouth buzz, positive audience scores (87% on Rotten Tomatoes), and broad appeal amid light competition. Prior leader Apex, a Charlize Theron survival thriller, has slipped to #4 despite 40 million prior weekly views, while newcomers like Remarkably Bright Creatures trail at #2. With the weekly chart (Monday-Sunday views) nearing lock on May 11, traders' skin-in-the-game consensus reflects Swapped's sustained momentum; realistic upsets would require an improbable late-week surge from holdovers or viral breakout by a challenger.

FK Pardubice enters as a narrow favorite at 41.5% implied probability, buoyed by strong away form (6 wins in 14) and recent head-to-head success, including 2-1 victories over MFK Karviná in October 2025 and March 2026. Karviná (36%) leverages home advantage at Městský stadion despite poor home record (5W-2D-8L), coming off a 0-3 loss to Mladá Boleslav, while Pardubice gained momentum from a 3-1 win versus Zlín last week. No major injuries reported, keeping lineups intact and the draw (34.5%) viable in this low-scoring H2H trend.

markets.question: Will SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 Superheavy explode?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the booster for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test explodes at any point during the test from the start of fueling operations to 60 minutes after it makes contact with Earth upon landing. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An explosion is defined as a violent and catastrophic event resulting in the destruction of all or part of the vehicle, regardless of intent or context (e.g., a planned termination event would also count).

If the twelfth launch has not occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: The sixth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight which exploded after executing controlled splashdown in the Gulf of Mexico would qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's Starship Flight Test 12 nears liftoff with the first-ever full stack of V3 Super Heavy Booster 19 and Ship 39 at Starbase's Orbital Launch Pad 2 on May 9, following a successful full-thrust 33-engine static fire of the booster on May 7 and the ship's full-duration static fire in mid-April. These milestones validate V3 upgrades for higher thrust, improved reusability, and faster turnaround, addressing prior flights' challenges like reentry heating and catch precision. FAA launch licenses span through October, with a NET window opening May 12 amid scrubbed wet dress rehearsals. Traders eye Pad 2's debut performance and potential weather or anomaly-driven slips, as rapid iteration defines SpaceX's path to orbital refueling and Mars missions.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Denmark's snap general election on March 24 produced a hung parliament, with Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats securing the most seats but failing to deliver a left-wing bloc majority, prompting extended coalition negotiations in the Folketing. Frederiksen's initial efforts to form a minority or coalition government collapsed this week, leading King Frederik X to task Deputy Prime Minister Troels Lund Poulsen of the center-right Venstre party with exploring a potential center-right administration on May 8. Lars Løkke Rasmussen's Moderates hold kingmaker status amid cross-bloc talks, driving trader consensus to price Frederiksen as the slim favorite at 56.5% implied probability, with Poulsen and Rasmussen as leading alternatives reflecting ongoing uncertainty and historical patterns of centrist coalitions. Traders await outcomes from these pivotal negotiations, which could resolve within weeks.

markets.question: Will Paloma Valencia place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

Late April surveys, including AtlasIntel (de la Espriella 29.4%, Valencia 20.9%) and Guarumo/EcoAnalítica (23.9% vs. 22.8%), show de la Espriella maintaining a slim edge despite Valencia's post-primary surge, reflecting trader consensus at 64.5% implied probability for his second-place finish amid 5-15% undecided voters. With the May 31 first round three weeks away, campaign momentum and security issues in rural areas could tip the balance for the opposition vote coordination.

eventMetadata.context_description: Santos FC, languishing in 18th place in the 2026 Brasileirão Série A after a dismal 1-5-5 start with just 8 points and a -7 goal difference through 11 matches, hosts mid-table Red Bull Bragantino (9th, 4-2-5, 14 points, +1 GD) at Estádio Urbano Caldeira on May 10. Santos desperately needs points in their relegation scrap, bolstered by home advantage and competitive head-to-head history—including a 2-2 draw last September—but faces injury woes with right-back Mayke (knee), midfielder Gabriel Menino (hamstring), and uncertainty over star forward Neymar following March muscle fatigue that sidelined him briefly. Bragantino contends with absences up front, including strikers Eduardo Sasha and Fernando (both ankle), potentially exposing defensive vulnerabilities on the road amid Santos' urgent momentum shift.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD hovers near 1.17, reflecting trader consensus on persistent US-Eurozone policy divergence as the primary driver, with the federal funds rate steady at 3.5%-3.75% versus the ECB deposit facility rate at 2.0%. Eurozone CPI surged to 3.0% in April from 2.6% in March, prompting ECB discussions of potential hikes despite its April 30 hold, while US CPI jumped to 3.3% YoY in March amid energy spikes. Higher-for-longer US rates bolster dollar strength, though recent weak consumer sentiment provided brief relief. Watch May CPI releases and the FOMC's June 16-17 meeting with dot plot updates for shifts in rate path expectations through year-end.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Istanbul be 19°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Istanbul Airport in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LTFM

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if LOS and Fluxo W7M play 4 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 4 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: LOS vs Fluxo W7M (BO5) - CBLOL Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between LOS and Fluxo W7M in the CBLOL Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 9 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LOS" if LOS win the match against Fluxo W7M.

This market will resolve to "Fluxo W7M" if Fluxo W7M win the match against LOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the CBLOL Split 1 2026 playoffs upper bracket round 1 BO5, 4th-seed LOS faces 5th-seed Fluxo W7M in a high-stakes rematch after LOS's 2-1 regular season victory over Fluxo just one week ago on May 3, where new jungler Curse debuted with strong macro plays to secure the comeback win. Both teams finished 4-3 in the regular season amid tight standings, with Fluxo showing resilience by tying that series 1-1 via midlaner cody's standout Zoe performance before faltering, while boasting recent upset wins over FURIA and LOUD. LOS holds a perfect recent head-to-head edge, but Fluxo's curty-Peach-cody-BAO-Momochi core thrives in extended series, making macro execution and draft adaptability pivotal in this double-elimination clash.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran diplomatic talks, mediated in Islamabad, Pakistan, remain stalled after marathon April sessions that failed to secure a ceasefire deal amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's nuclear program, and US sanctions. Recent US strikes on Iranian targets have tested a fragile truce, but as of early May, envoy Steve Witkoff reported resumed conversations, with Iran reviewing a one-page US proposal for de-escalation—though President Trump called it likely unacceptable. Prior delegations featured US Vice President JD Vance alongside Witkoff and Jared Kushner opposite Iranian Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and Abbas Araghchi; similar lineups are anticipated for any imminent next round, potentially within days, as both sides weigh escalation risks.

markets.question: Will "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office be between 40m and 45m?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to how much "Mortal Kombat II" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (May 8 - May 10) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-05 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-05 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Absent major shifts before the June 1 filing deadline or August 11 primaries, the district's conservative suburban base around Waukesha sustains the lopsided odds, with no notable developments in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Takaichi and Jinping are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's hawkish stance, including late 2025 remarks on potential military involvement in a Taiwan contingency, continues to strain China-Japan relations six months later, with Beijing maintaining a hardline posture and rejecting Tokyo's recent overtures for a summit at the 2026 APEC gathering. No in-person meeting between Takaichi and President Xi Jinping has occurred since their October 2025 APEC sideline talks, amid ongoing disputes over Senkaku/Diaoyu islands and rare earth exports. Takaichi's February 2026 election victory bolstered her security agenda, prompting alliances with Australia and Vietnam, while trader consensus reflects uncertainty over bilateral diplomacy before year-end, potentially catalyzed by multilateral forums like G20 or APEC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Club Libertad hosts Independiente del Valle in Copa Libertadores Group H at Estadio Tigo La Huerta, where trader consensus prices reflect a razor-thin contest at 47% apiece for victory and 44% for draw, underscoring balanced strengths amid mutual vulnerabilities. Libertad benefits from home advantage and a recent 3-2 league win but grapples with poor form—no victories in five prior matches—and key absences like ligament-injured Alexis Duarte and Hugo Fernández, plus questionable Hugo Fernández. markets.question: Iran leadership change by June 30?

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, appointed after his father Ali Khamenei's assassination in a February 28 Israeli strike, faces persistent health challenges from injuries sustained in the same attack, including facial burns, leg surgeries, and potential prosthetic needs, keeping him out of public view since early March. US intelligence assesses him as mentally sharp but isolated, communicating via couriers while the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) dominates wartime decision-making through a collective leadership model with the Supreme National Security Council. President Masoud Pezeshkian's recent meeting confirms his involvement, yet power fragmentation amid ongoing Israel-Iran hostilities and stalled Hormuz Strait talks fuels trader uncertainty over further transitions. No scheduled Assembly of Experts sessions loom, but escalation or diplomatic breakthroughs could shift dynamics before year-end.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Labour's heavy losses in recent local elections, where Reform UK surged to gain over 1,400 seats and control of 14 councils, have intensified scrutiny on Prime Minister Keir Starmer ahead of the next Prime Minister's Questions, likely on Wednesday, May 13, coinciding with the State Opening of Parliament and King's Speech marking a new session after last week's prorogation. Opposition leader Kemi Badenoch is expected to press Starmer on voter discontent, welfare spending clashes from the prior PMQs on April 29, and the ongoing Peter Mandelson nomination controversy involving Foreign Office vetting delays and security clearance issues tied to past associations. Starmer has responded with vows to fight on, outlining cost-of-living relief plans including potential fuel duty cuts and closer European ties via a youth mobility scheme by 2027, amid broader pressures from Iran tensions and Ukraine. Traders should monitor the official Hansard transcript for exact phrasing in Starmer's replies to these topical questions.
